title: 10 best herbal teas for nausea
intro_desc: Herbal teas for nausea are infusions made from various plants and spices that can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. They are a natural and gentle alternative to over-the-counter medications and have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. Some examples of herbal teas that help with nausea include ginger, peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root. Ginger tea is well-known for its anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe the stomach and reduce feelings of nausea. Peppermint tea has calming effects on the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and promoting digestion. Chamomile tea contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help relax the stomach muscles and alleviate nausea. Licorice root tea can help soothe the mucous membranes of the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and irritation. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can find relief from nausea and improve their overall quality of life, especially during times of stress, motion sickness, or pregnancy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with nausea because it contains menthol, a compound that has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce the likelihood of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, peppermint tea has been shown to improve digestion and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can also help alleviate feelings of nausea. Furthermore, the soothing warmth of the tea can help calm a queasy stomach and provide a sense of relaxation, making it a popular natural remedy for nausea.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Peppermint tea helps relieve nausea by relaxing gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and promoting the flow of bile, which is facilitated by active constituents like menthol and methyl salicylate.

Carminative: Its ability to reduce gas and bloating makes peppermint tea an effective nausea remedy, as these symptoms often accompany or worsen nausea, due to volatile oils like menthol and menthone.
Anesthetic: Peppermint tea can numb the lining of the stomach, reducing the sensation of nausea, thanks to the mild anesthetic properties of menthol and menthone.
Antiemetic: Peppermint tea may help alleviate nausea by reducing the likelihood of vomiting, as it contains antiemetic compounds like menthol and rosmarinic acid.

Relaxation: Peppermint tea promotes relaxation and stress reduction, which can help alleviate nausea caused by anxiety or stress, due to the calming effects of volatile oils like menthol and limonene.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for nausea due to their calming and soothing effects on the stomach.
Flowers: Peppermint flowers are used in tea to alleviate nausea, as they contain menthol which helps relax the gastrointestinal tract.
Seeds: Peppermint seeds are rich in essential oils, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas to treat nausea.
Stems: The stems of the peppermint plant contain therapeutic compounds, making them a useful addition to teas for treating nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with nausea because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. This can help alleviate the symptoms of nausea, including stomach discomfort and vomiting. Additionally, licorice tea can help stimulate the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, which can protect the stomach lining and further reduce nausea. The tea's warm temperature can also help relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, providing additional relief from nausea and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can cause nausea.
Demulcent: The tea's demulcent properties, from substances like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, form a soothing film over the mucous membranes, protecting the stomach and reducing nausea.
Mucosal protective: Licorice tea's mucosal protective effect, owing to glycyrrhizin, helps maintain the stomach's mucosal barrier, preventing irritation and subsequent nausea.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties, attributed to glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help relax the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating nausea caused by spasms or cramps.
Adaptogenic: Licorice tea's adaptogenic properties, resulting from compounds like glycyrrhizin, help the body cope with stress and anxiety, common triggers of nausea.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for every 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root.
Add the dried licorice root to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes have been used in traditional medicine to treat nausea and other digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant can be used to make tea that can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and indigestion.
Seeds: Licorice seeds contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant can be used to make tea that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with nausea because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which work to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. These compounds also help to relax the smooth muscle of the intestines, which can help to alleviate feelings of nausea and promote healthy digestion. Additionally, ginger tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help to break down food in the stomach and reduce feelings of discomfort. The soothing warmth of ginger tea can also help to calm a queasy stomach and provide relief from nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Ginger tea helps reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the serotonin receptors in the gut, which are involved in triggering vomiting. The active constituents responsible for this property are 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.

Gastric motility improvement: Ginger tea enhances gastric motility, helping to move food and digestive enzymes through the digestive system more efficiently, which can alleviate feelings of nausea. The primary active compounds responsible for this property are 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.

Delayed gastric emptying reduction: Ginger tea helps reduce delayed gastric emptying, which can contribute to nausea and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this effect are 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.

Inflammation reduction: Ginger tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated gastrointestinal tissues, reducing nausea. The primary active compounds responsible for this property are 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.

Anxiety reduction: Ginger tea may help reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to nausea. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils like zingiberene, β-bisabolene, and α-farnesene.
remedy_recipe: Peel and chop 1-2 inches of fresh ginger root into small pieces.
Place ginger pieces in a pot and add 2 cups of water.
Bring water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the ginger pieces.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink while warm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots are commonly used to make medicinal tea for nausea due to their natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that help soothe the digestive system.

Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes, a horizontal stem that grows underground, are used in tea to alleviate nausea because they contain volatile oils and phenol compounds that aid in digestion and have calming effects.
Flowers: Ginger flowers are sometimes used in teas to help with nausea, as they contain antioxidants that support overall digestive health and may help reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Ginger seeds can be used to make tea, and they contain compounds that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with nausea because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system and alleviate feelings of sickness. The mild flavor and lack of added ingredients make it a gentle choice for those with sensitive stomachs. Additionally, white tea has been shown to stimulate the digestive system and promote the secretion of digestive enzymes, which can help to break down food and reduce the likelihood of nausea. Overall, white tea's natural soothing properties make it an effective and natural remedy for nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce irritation in the stomach lining, which may contribute to nausea. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, particularly catechins.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in white tea can help protect cells from damage, which may help reduce nausea caused by oxidative stress. This is attributed to the polyphenols found in white tea.
Antiemetic: White tea has been shown to have antiemetic properties, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting. This is due to the presence of tannins and volatile oils.
Gastroprotective: White tea's gastroprotective properties can help safeguard the stomach lining, reducing the likelihood of nausea. This is attributed to the flavonoids and alkaloids in white tea.
Calming: White tea can have a calming effect on the body, which may help reduce feelings of nausea. This is due to the presence of L-theanine, an amino acid that promotes relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 160-180°F (71-82°C), as boiling water can damage the delicate tea leaves and reduce its health benefits.
Add 1 teaspoon (about 2 grams) of dried white tea leaves or 1 tea bag to a cup or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the tea leaves and let it steep for 1-3 minutes, depending on how strong you prefer the tea.
Optional: Add 1/2 inch (1.25 cm) of fresh ginger root, sliced, or 1/4 teaspoon of ground ginger to the tea for added nausea relief.
Strain the tea leaves or remove the tea bag, and enjoy the warm tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: White tea leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea.
Buds: The unopened buds of the white tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants and are believed to help settle the stomach and reduce feelings of nausea.
Rhyzomes: Although not as common, the rhyzomes of some white tea plants have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with nausea because it contains antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce the intensity and frequency of nausea. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have calming effects on the stomach, which can help alleviate feelings of queasiness and promote overall digestive health. Its soothing aroma and mild, lemony flavor also make it a pleasant and comforting beverage to sip on when experiencing nausea. Furthermore, lemon balm tea is caffeine-free and gentle on the stomach, making it a safe and natural remedy for nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic effect: Lemon balm tea can help reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the actions of chemicals that cause these symptoms (active constituents
Carminative effect: It helps expel gas from the stomach and intestines, alleviating bloating and discomfort that can worsen nausea (active constituents
Anxiolytic effect: Lemon balm tea can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which may help alleviate nausea caused by stress or anxiety (active constituent
Spasmolytic effect: It can help relieve gastrointestinal spasms and relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing nausea caused by muscle contractions (active constituents
Antioxidant effect: Lemon balm tea has antioxidant properties that may help protect the gastrointestinal tract from damage and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to nausea (active constituents
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's medicinal properties.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, leaving only the liquid.
Optional: Add honey or lemon juice to taste for improved flavor.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in teas for nausea due to their calming and soothing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm are used in teas for nausea because they contain volatile oils that have antispasmodic and carminative effects.
Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of lemon balm can also be included in teas for nausea as they contain similar medicinal properties as the leaves and flowers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with nausea because it contains volatile oils, such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole, which have antispasmodic and carminative properties. These compounds help to relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing symptoms of bloating, gas, and cramps that can contribute to feelings of nausea. Additionally, fennel tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help to speed up the digestion process and alleviate discomfort in the stomach. The soothing and aromatic nature of fennel tea can also help to calm the mind and reduce stress, which can be a contributing factor to nausea. Overall, fennel tea offers a natural and effective way to relieve nausea and promote healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel tea can help to relieve nausea by reducing bloating and gas, thanks to its active constituents, such as anethole and fenchone, which help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles.

Antispasmodic: Fennel tea can help to soothe the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea, due to the presence of volatile oils like anethole, limonene, and alpha-pinene.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating nausea caused by gastritis or other inflammatory conditions. The active constituents responsible for these effects include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils.

Antiemetic: Fennel tea can help to reduce nausea by directly acting on the vomiting center in the brain, thanks to its active constituents like anethole and fenchone, which can help to regulate the nervous system and reduce the sensation of nausea.

Digestive stimulant: Fennel tea can help to improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help to reduce nausea caused by indigestion or malabsorption. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils like anethole, limonene, and alpha-pinene, which can help to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried fennel seeds.
Boil 1 cup of water and add the fennel seeds.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the fennel seeds.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in medicinal teas for nausea due to their carminative properties that help to reduce bloating and gas.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have antispasmodic qualities that can help soothe an upset stomach and alleviate feelings of nausea.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain volatile oils that can help stimulate digestion and provide relief from nausea and other gastrointestinal symptoms.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the fennel plant have a mild flavor and are often used in teas to help settle the stomach and alleviate nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tea helps with nausea because it contains carminative and antispasmodic properties that help to soothe the digestive tract, reducing feelings of queasiness and vomiting. The volatile oils found in caraway seeds, such as carvone and limonene, stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help to break down food and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. Additionally, caraway tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to nausea. Its mild flavor and aroma make it a pleasant and soothing beverage that can be enjoyed throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Caraway tea helps to relieve nausea by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles and reducing bloating, thanks to the active constituents, primarily, carvol and carvone.
Antiemetic property: Caraway tea can help alleviate nausea and vomiting by blocking the signals that trigger these symptoms, due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.
Spasmolytic property: Caraway tea can help soothe the stomach and intestinal spasms, which can contribute to nausea, as a result of the relaxing effects of compounds such as carvone and limonene.

Antiflatulent property: Caraway tea helps reduce gas and bloating, which can cause or worsen nausea, by promoting the expulsion of excess gas from the digestive tract, due to its active constituents, particularly carvol and carvone.

Stomachic property: Caraway tea can help stimulate appetite and promote digestion, which can be beneficial for nausea caused by a lack of appetite or digestive issues, due to the presence of various volatile oils and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried caraway seeds.
Place the seeds in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the caraway seeds.
Steep the tea for 10 minutes, then remove the infuser or strain out the seeds.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach as needed.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal teas for nausea due to their carminative properties that help relieve digestive discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway plant have antispasmodic properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and alleviate feelings of nausea.
Fruits: The fruits of the caraway plant, also known as seeds, are rich in essential oils and have been used traditionally to treat nausea and other digestive issues.
Rhizomes: Caraway rhizomes contain antiemetic compounds that can help reduce nausea and vomiting, making them a popular ingredient in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tea helps with nausea because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and relieve digestive discomfort. The menthol present in spearmint can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing the likelihood of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, spearmint tea has been shown to have calming effects on the mind and body, which can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and stress that may contribute to nausea. Its refreshing and mildly sweet flavor can also help hydrate the body and promote saliva production, providing further relief from nausea.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic: Spearmint tea contains antiemetic properties that help reduce the likelihood of nausea and vomiting by regulating the activity of the digestive system, thanks to the presence of menthol and limonene.

Carminative: Spearmint tea has carminative properties that help alleviate nausea caused by gas and bloating by promoting the expulsion of intestinal gas, due to the presence of menthol and flavonoids.

Antispasmodic: Spearmint tea contains antispasmodic properties that help soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea caused by muscle spasms in the digestive tract, thanks to the presence of rosmarinic acid and menthol.
Anti-inflammatory: Spearmint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea caused by inflammation in the digestive tract, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Anxiolytic: Spearmint tea contains anxiolytic properties that help reduce nausea caused by anxiety and stress by promoting relaxation and reducing tension, thanks to the presence of menthol and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried spearmint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the spearmint leaves, retaining only the liquid.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the herbal spearmint tea slowly to alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Spearmint leaves are rich in antioxidants and menthol, which can help soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds contain essential oils that can aid in digestion and reduce nausea symptoms.
Flowers: Spearmint flowers have calming properties that can help relax the stomach muscles and alleviate feelings of nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with nausea because it contains antispasmodic properties that help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of nausea and vomiting. Its calming effects on the stomach can also help alleviate feelings of discomfort and promote overall digestive health. Furthermore, chamomile tea has been shown to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the digestion of fats and can help prevent nausea caused by indigestion. Additionally, its mild sedative properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can often contribute to feelings of nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: German chamomile tea contains anti-emetic properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting, important for managing symptoms of various conditions such as motion sickness and digestive issues. Active constituents include chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol.

Carminative: The carminative property of German chamomile tea helps alleviate gas and bloating, which can contribute to feelings of nausea. This is achieved through the presence of volatile oils like bisabolol and matricin.

Spasmolytic: German chamomile tea has spasmolytic properties that help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause nausea. This is attributed to the flavonoid apigenin.

Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, potentially alleviating nausea caused by inflammatory conditions. Active constituents include the terpenoids azulene and alpha-bisabolol.

Anxiolytic: German chamomile tea has anxiolytic properties that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate nausea caused by stress or anxiety. The flavonoid apigenin is responsible for this effect.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the chamomile flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to discard the spent flowers.
Drink the herbal tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day, to alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in calming compounds, making them effective in soothing digestive issues like nausea.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and gentle on the stomach, chamomile leaves can help alleviate nausea and promote overall digestive health.
Seeds: Often used in tea blends, chamomile seeds contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and support a healthy gastrointestinal tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tea helps with nausea because it contains a variety of herbal ingredients known for their soothing and calming properties. Peppermint, one of the key ingredients, has been shown to have antispasmodic effects on the gastrointestinal tract, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting. Ginger, another important component, is a well-known natural remedy for digestive issues, including nausea, due to its ability to neutralize stomach acids and promote the release of digestive enzymes. Chamomile, lemongrass, and other herbs in Eureka tea also contribute to its effectiveness in alleviating nausea, making it a gentle and natural option for those seeking relief from this unpleasant symptom.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Eureka tea contains alkaloids such as berberine and caffeine, which are known to suppress nausea and vomiting by blocking the signals that trigger these symptoms.
Carminative: The tea's essential oils, like limonene and pinene, help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing nausea caused by gas and bloating.
Antispasmodic: Eureka tea has antispasmodic properties due to its content of flavonoids and tannins, which soothe the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, alleviating nausea from cramps and spasms.

Anti-inflammatory: The presence of phenolic compounds and terpenes in Eureka tea helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, minimizing nausea and discomfort associated with gastrointestinal issues.
Adaptogenic: Eureka tea's adaptogenic properties, attributed to its diverse phytochemicals, help regulate the body's stress response, thereby mitigating stress-induced nausea.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of fresh gingerly slices, 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves, and 1.5 liters of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 tablespoon of lemon juice and 1 tablespoon of honey, then stir well.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the eureka plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for nausea due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the eureka plant are used in teas for nausea because they contain calming compounds that can help reduce stomach irritation.
Seeds: Eureka plant seeds are rich in oils and compounds that can help settle the stomach and alleviate nausea when consumed as a tea.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the eureka plant are used in medicinal teas for nausea due to their ability to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce symptoms of nausea.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nausea with herbal teas is by choosing teas with soothing properties. Peppermint and ginger teas are excellent choices. Peppermint has menthol, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, potentially reducing nausea. Ginger, on the other hand, is a well-known remedy for digestive issues, including nausea, due to its gingerols and shaogals content, which are known to help calm the stomach. Always steep the tea in hot water for the recommended time to ensure the release of beneficial compounds. However, remember that while herbal teas can aid in alleviating nausea, they should not replace medical advice or treatment, especially for severe or persistent nausea.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nausea that are treated with herbal teas include indigestion, stomach cramps, and motion sickness. Peppermint tea is commonly used to soothe digestive issues, while ginger tea is well-known for alleviating nausea, vomiting, and dizziness associated with motion sickness. Chamomile tea can help relax the stomach muscles, reducing cramps and spasms. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy, especially for pregnant women or those with pre-existing medical conditions.
